name: Personal Tax & Finance
description: Understand how taxes work, how to file, and how to make informed financial decisions

concepts.name: Why Taxes Exist
concepts.description: What taxes fund, how government revenue works, and the social contract behind taxation.

concepts.name: Types of Income
concepts.description: W-2 wages, 1099 self-employment, investment income, and how each is taxed differently.

concepts.name: Tax Brackets & Marginal Rates
concepts.description: How progressive taxation works. Why moving into a higher bracket doesn't mean all your income is taxed at that rate.

concepts.name: Deductions vs Credits
concepts.description: Deductions reduce taxable income; credits reduce tax owed. Standard deduction vs itemizing.

concepts.name: Filing Status
concepts.description: Single, married filing jointly, head of household — how status affects brackets and standard deduction.

concepts.name: W-4 & Withholding
concepts.description: How employers withhold taxes from each paycheck, and how to adjust your W-4 to avoid owing or over-withholding.

concepts.name: Filing a Tax Return
concepts.description: What forms you need (W-2, 1099), choosing software or a preparer, and the filing timeline.

concepts.name: Retirement Accounts & Taxes
concepts.description: Traditional vs Roth IRAs and 401(k)s. Tax-deferred vs tax-free growth and contribution limits.

concepts.name: Capital Gains Tax
concepts.description: Short-term vs long-term capital gains. How selling investments, property, or assets triggers taxable events.

concepts.name: Self-Employment Tax
concepts.description: How freelancers and contractors pay both the employee and employer share of Social Security and Medicare.
